root_text.sn47.44:1.4: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kāye kāyānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ;
root_text.sn47.44:1.7: dhammesu dhammānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn47.44:1.8: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sato hoti.

translation_text.sn47.44:1.2: This is my instruction to you.
translation_text.sn47.44:1.3: And how is a mendicant mindful?
translation_text.sn47.44:1.4: It’s when a mendicant meditates by observing an aspect of the body—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.44:1.5: They meditate observing an aspect of feelings …
translation_text.sn47.44:1.7: principles—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.44:1.8: That’s how a mendicant is mindful.
translation_text.sn47.44:1.10: This is my instruction to you.”
